pages.raw_text:
The Central Labor union last
night received a letter from the
secretary of the City Baseball
league which stated that that or
ganization had not definitely set
tied on grounds for the coming
season. The league used the
grounds of the Washington Water
Power Co. at Natatorium park last
summer. The various unions recog-
nized the fact that the league had
an arrangement for the use of the
grounds prior to the placing of the
park on the unfair list and little
effort was made to keep members
of local unions from playing on the
teams. No demand was made on
any club towithdraw from the
league.


This season the unions propose to
continue the fight against the big
power monopoly as vigorously as
ever and an effort will be made to
secure grounds elsewhere for the
use of the City league teams.


A committee was appointed last
night to wait on Mr.. Graves and
other officers of the Traction Co.
and endeavor to secure grounds
near Mantle park for the use of the
amateurs. 1t is pointed out that
the professional team will be away
about half the time and that lovers
of baseball among working men
would crowd the stands to see the
City league play providing they did
not have to patronize the cars OF
the Washington Water Power Co.


The Central Labor Union devoted
the greater portion of the evening
to nominating officers to be elected
next Monday night. Nominations
will remain open until the next
meeting. The engineers asked that
the advertising matter of Tefft &
Schaffer be removed as lar as was
possible from conspicuous places
where union men were employed
This referred to barber shops and
like places where the ice rink was
being advertised.


A large list of nominations were
made and interest in the election
will be keen.
